page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-4 is an arm64 general-purpose server powered by an ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 4 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, 16.0 GB of RAM, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography but no advanced vector or matrix extensions.

Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as large-block memory bandwidth. However, it exhibits weak performance in small-block cached memory reads, software compilation, and text processing. Cryptographic performance is limited, with the Passmark encryption test scoring in the bottom 10%, which correlates with the absence of AVX and AMX instruction sets.

Qualitatively, the t2a-standard-4 offers a resource-dense profile for ARM-native workloads. Given its average database operations performance and weak web serving and Redis throughput, this instance is best suited for lightweight multi-threaded applications, basic database tasks, and general-purpose ARM development rather than high-throughput web serving or intensive LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-4 is an arm64 general-purpose server featuring 4 dedicated physical cores on an ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz. Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, this instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities in stress-ng CPU tests and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside weak performance in web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation. Lacking advanced vector and matrix extensions, its cryptographic and LLM inference performance is limited, with Passmark encryption scoring in the bottom 10%. The t2a-standard-4 represents a qualitatively cost-efficient entry point for ARM-native development, lightweight database operations, and general-purpose multi-threaded workloads that do not require high-throughput memory or specialized instruction sets.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-4 is an arm64 general-purpose server with 4 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores, 16 GB RAM, and average CPU performance, suited for lightweight database tasks and ARM development.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-4 is an arm64 general-purpose server featuring 4 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and 16 GB of RAM.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores for balanced, lightweight multi-threaded workloads and ARM-native development.
bullet_points: Powered by an ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz
Features 4 dedicated physical cores and 16.0 GB of system memory
Delivers average CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Lacks local storage, integrated GPUs, and advanced vector extensions
Best suited for general-purpose ARM development and lightweight database tasks
